title: Resinous Greek Eggplant Gigantes Plaki
description: A springy, resinous eggplant stew infused with poppy seeds and a silky tomato-olive emulsion, perfect for a romantic date night served in a bento box.
instructions: Slice the eggplant into 2 cm thick rounds, sprinkle with salt, and let them sit for 20 minutes to draw out bitterness.
Rinse and pat dry the eggplant slices, brush each side with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, and roast them in a preheated 200°C oven for 25 minutes until golden.
While the eggplant roasts, finely dice the onion and mince the garlic, then sauté them in a pan with 1 tablespoon olive oil until translucent.
Add the canned tomato sauce, fresh oregano, lemon juice, poppy seeds, salt, pepper, and 100 ml water to the pan, simmer for 15 minutes to create a thick tomato emulsion.
Assemble the dish by layering roasted eggplant slices in a bento box compartment, spooning the tomato sauce over them, and repeating until all ingredients are used.
Cover the bento box with foil and let it rest for 10 minutes to allow flavors to mingle and the sauce to thicken.
Garnish the finished platter with a sprinkle of poppy seeds and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil before serving.
Serve the resinous Greek eggplant Gigantes Plaki warm, ideally with a side of crusty bread or a light salad.
